rationale: The cited paper appears in the literature review as a single prior example: it is briefly described as studying entrants' innovate-versus-imitate choice relative to incumbent R&D. It is listed alongside other background references to position the paper's contribution, but the citing paper does not build its model, assumptions, derivations, or tests directly on that specific study. There is no distinct downstream use of the cited paper beyond contextual framing in a grouped citation discussion.
